strategy: agentic chunking via Gemini (headline + summary + original text)

pages.text:
BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2015

Personal Training Client Application Form

The more information you provide, the better I will be able to assist you. If you have
any questions with regards to this form, please do not hesitate to contact me. You
can use whichever measurement system (e.g. metric, imperial) you prefer to fill in
this form. Just state which unit you used (e.g. cm, kg, lb, inch).

 Full legal name: Matthew
 Full billing address: US
 Age: 27
 Height: 6ft 3in
 Weight: 180
 Body fat percentage (plus measurement technique): 16.5% as of 3/6/15 (DXA)
 Years of training experience: < 1 year
For the section below, please give your current maximum abilities in weight times
reps, e.g. 250 lb x 6.
 Bench press:
 Squat (excluding body weight):
 Chin-up (excluding body weight): Bodyweight only approximately 3 chin ups
 Overhead press:
I do not currently have a max established for any of the above workouts. I have
not been resistance training in the gym with free weights on a consistent basis for
years. However, I am ready to go get this information as soon as possible.
June of 2014 I started a very low carb diet (keto) with a starting weight of 240. At
the end of January 2015 I weighed 175. At the beginning of February I started
P90x3 as a "kick start" if you will to get back in the gym. I feel better and my body
composition has improved, the workout is every day for 30 minutes. It is a mix of
cardio, resistance training, core, and balance. While I enjoy the at home workouts
I am ready to hit the gym and do what it takes to reach my goal. I have come this
far and won't be going back.

BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2015

What are your fitness goals? I would like to lose body fat. I have been overweight for
much of my life. I still have some belly fat that I am hoping to lose. From the research
I have done I won't get a "flat" belly until around 10% body fat. I read your article on
bodybuilding vs. aesthetics and I am somewhat going for the physique Brad Pitt has
in Fight Club (I am realistic but it was a really good example you gave).

How often in a week would you be prepared to train, if this was the optimal training
frequency?

I would be willing to train 7 days a week

What is your occupation? You may be general here, e.g. student/manager/personal
trainer/manual labor/doctor. The aim is to characterize your circadian rhythm,
stress and activity level.
I am self-employed. Normally wake up between 7-9am CST and sleep around
midnight.

Please list any pathologies and injuries (disabilities, allergies, illnesses, syndromes,
disorders etc.) you have or had:

Do you plan to use any PEDs while working together? If so, please list them:
Had not planned on it

BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2015

Activity level: please mark one of the fields below or delete the rest.
 Sedentary (e.g. office job) Sedentary (30 minutes a day of mild exercise for
past two months)
Stress level: please mark one of the fields below or delete the rest.
 Only occasional/mild stress (e.g. student not during exam period)

How is your sleep rhythm and quality?
I have not had a professional sleep study done. I would say I wake up around 10 times
a night and actually get up one time. I use a Microsoft Band and it says I usually get
under an hour of "restful sleep" between 5 and 6 hours of "light" sleep with
approximately 15 "wake-ups"

How much caffeine do you consume daily on average or on a typical work day?
480mg (Cellucor SuperHD supplement)

Do you have skinfold calipers and if so, which one(s)?

If you answered yes to the question on PED usage, you can leave the section below
blank.
At this point, you may choose to return this form to me to obtain a price quote
before filling in the rest. Please attach at least two full-body pictures of yourself in clear
lighting, preferably including a back double biceps and front relaxed pose.

Legal disclaimer: By returning this intake form, you declare that while Menno
Henselmans is your personal trainer, all the substances you use are obtained and used in
accordance with their governing laws and you forego all rights to hold Menno
Henselmans liable for any damages caused in relation to following his advice as your
trainer.

pages.text:
BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2015

 Wrist circumference: 18cm
 Ankle circumference: 25.5cm
 How much did you weigh before you started lifting and what was your height
at that point? 240 lb 6ft 3 in
 Were you heavy as a baby? Yes
 Please attach a picture to this form in which the back of your right hand is
clearly visible. Ideally, place your right hand on a flat surface with your fingers
touching each other and then take a picture of it.

What are the lightest barbell weight plates you have access to? 2.5lbs

What are the smallest weight increments between the sets of dumbbells you have
access to?

Do you have access to…
a squat cage or rack? Yes
a 45° hyperextension bench? Yes
a reverse hyper machine? Yes
a glute-ham raise? Yes
a standing calf raise machine? Yes
a dip/chin-up belt? No
a leg curl machine and if so, is it seated, lying or standing? Seated and Lying (if I
remember my local gym correctly)
a pair of knee wraps? No
gymnastic rings or a TRX? No
powerlifting bands or chains? No

BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2015

a seated calf raise where you push your body up (closed chain: see example image
below)? Yes

Does your available equipment differ from that in most gyms in any other way (e.g.
you train in a Crossfit box or at home)? No

Please list all the supplements you are currently taking:
Cellucor SuperHD, T3 fuel (Just started this), Isopure protein powder, 6mg of
Nicotine gum (2mg in morning before workout with SuperHD, 2mg after morning
exercise, 2mg with second dosage of Cellucor SuperHD in afternoon.

Please describe or attach your current diet plan in detail: I have been on a Keto diet
since June 2014. I tried to stick to 5/45/50 of C/P/F for macros. I was losing weight
so well in the beginning I didn't keep track of calories very much. My fat loss seems to
have stalled for 2015. I would say I have probably been averaging 2,000 calories a
day. I log my macros as much as possible. Some days I will eat 3,000 calories (still
under 30g carb) some days I will eat 2,000 calories or less, still staying under 30g. In
March I started implementing a cheat day/refeed/carb load for 6-8 hours. My weight
will usually go up and then back to what it was before the refeed by the time the next
refeed comes along.

Just this week I switched to eating 150g of protein a day, under 20g carbs, rest fat.

BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2015

1330 calories on rest days. 2030 calories on resistance days. Same protein and carbs
every day.

Please describe or attach your current training program in detail:
As I have mentioned above I currently am training with p90x3. I normally wake up
and train on an empty stomach. An hour after training I will consume a protein shake
with 20-35 grams of protein (usually around 11am). Around 3 or 4 I take another
Cellucor Supplement with 2mg of nicotine. Around 7 I will have something like
chicken wings or steak. Ranges from 655 calories t0 1300. 45g of fat and 60g of
protein for the lower end. 67.5g of fat and 90g protein on the high end. I am usually
in the middle. This is my new plan, I will normally have something small if needed to
hit my protein macro or have a small protein shake before bed. On the days I eat
more I usually have a 1/2lb burger included for lunch. Those are the days my calories
go as high as 2500 or 3000 (excluding my carb load)

Are there any times at which you are unable or unwilling to train? If so, please list
them:
Not really

Please return the filled in form to mennohenselmans@bayesianbodybuilding.com
and attach at least two full-body pictures of yourself in clear lighting (without
excessive shadows), preferably including a back double biceps and front relaxed
pose. If you recently had blood work done, you can attach its results as well.

Given that the client is a novice that will need to drastically change his lifestyle
around and his anabolic window is likely around 24 h, we can use a lenient and
simple diet design. This is the exact diet I sent him (zoom in with ctrl + scroll). He's
eating 4 meals a day.

BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2015

Your diet is simple in nature. After each training session your next 4 meals are training day meals.
After that all your meals are rest meals until your next training session.
You have a considerable degree of leeway with your macros on training days.
Training meals
Daily average
Per meal
The following error margins are acceptable.
Protein Intake
180
45
Fat Intake
71
18
Protein
Fat
Carbohydrate
Calories
Carb Intake
90
23
Daily total
+50%
-3% / + 50%
-50%
+/-1%
Kcal
1719
430
Per meal
+50%
+/-50%
+/-50%
+/-20%
Rest meals
Daily average
Per meal
Your rest/cut days are protein sparing modified fasts (PSMFs).
Protein Intake
180
45
This means you should aim to minimize caloric intake.
Kcal
1494
374
This effectively means your calories are maxima and your protein intake is a minimum.
Don't worry about the carb:fat ratio.
For satiety, it tends to help to either keep net carbs under 10 g per meal or to minimize fat intake.
